content: read reviews/625.md. if the summary section of the checklist review indicates the review passed, do nothing. if it failed, make relevant updates that are needed to deepmind/625.lean accordingly. if in doubt, reference CHECKLIST.md, which was the source document used when a previous instance of claude produced reviews/625.md.

1→# Formal Conjectures PR Checklist — `deepmind/625.lean`
     2→
     3→Every item must pass before a PR is approved. Reviewers: reject if any box is unchecked
     4→without an explicit justification.
     5→
     6→---
     7→
     8→## 1. Copyright Header
     9→
    10→- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
    11→- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.
    12→
    13→## 2. Import Statement
    14→
    15→- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports`.
    16→- [x] No other imports are present.
    17→
    18→## 3. Module Docstring
    19→
    20→- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
    21→- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erdős Problem 625`.
    22→- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to `erdosproblems.com/625`.
    23→- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem (defines cochromatic number ζ(G) and chromatic number χ(G), asks whether χ(G) − ζ(G) → ∞ almost surely for G(n, 1/2)).
    24→- [x] Bibliography entry present: `[ErGi93]` with author names and title.
    25→
    26→## 4. Namespace
    27→
    28→- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos625 ... end Erdos625`.
    29→- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase` (`Erdos625`).
    30→- [x] `open` statements appear **before** the namespace block: `open SimpleGraph Finset` (line 36).
    31→
    32→## 5. Custom Definitions
    33→
    34→- [x] `toGraph625` (line 43) has a LaTeX docstring (lines 40–42).
    35→- [x] `finChromaticNumber` (line 51) has a LaTeX docstring (lines 48–50).
    36→- [x] `cochromaticNumber` (line 57) has a LaTeX docstring (lines 54–56).
    37→- [x] `finChromaticNumber` is marked `noncomputable` (uses `sInf`, involving classical choice).
    38→- [x] `cochromaticNumber` is marked `noncomputable` (uses `sInf`, involving classical choice).
    39→- [x] `toGraph625` is computable (only uses `Bool` operations and decidable comparisons) — no `noncomputable` needed.
    40→- [x] No `sorry` in any definition.
    41→- N/A: No `Type`/`Prop` definitions to check for `UpperCamelCase`.
    42→- [x] Functions returning values use `lowerCamelCase`: `toGraph625`, `finChromaticNumber`, `cochromaticNumber`.
    43→- N/A: No `local notation` used (none needed).
    44→- N/A: No `@[category test]`/`@[category API]` sanity checks (optional; "consider" item).
    45→
    46→## 6. Theorem Statements
    47→
    48→- [x] Uses the `theorem` keyword.
    49→- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_625` (snake_case, matches namespace number).
    50→- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research open, AMS 5 60]`.
    51→- [x] Main theorem has a docstring (lines 63–73) describing the problem and its formal interpretation.
    52→- [x] Theorem docstring references source paper: `[ErGi93]`.
    53→
    54→### `answer()` usage
    55→
    56→The original problem asks "Is it true that almost surely χ(G) − ζ(G) → ∞?" — a yes/no question ("Is it true that ..."), so **Case 1** applies.
    57→
    58→- [x] Theorem type is `answer(sorry) ↔ P` (line 75).
    59→- [x] The RHS (`P`) formalizes the question as originally asked: for every M : ℕ and ε > 0, there exists n₀ such that for all n ≥ n₀, the fraction of Boolean edge matrices on `Fin n` whose graph has χ − ζ ≥ M is at least (1 − ε) times the total count. This correctly captures "almost surely χ − ζ → ∞" via the combinatorial/measure interpretation.
    60→- [x] `answer(True)` would mean "yes" and `answer(False)` would mean "no" to the original question.
    61→- [x] Quantifiers go **after** `answer(sorry)` (inside the RHS of `↔`), not as theorem parameters.
    62→
    63→## 7. Category Attribute
    64→
    65→- [x] Exactly one `@[category ...]` attribute: `category research open`.
    66→- [x] `research open` is a valid category value.
    67→- [x] Theorem contains `sorry` in its proof body (consistent with `research open`).
    68→
    69→## 8. AMS Attribute
    70→
    71→- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute: `AMS 5 60`.
    72→- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed (two: 5 and 60).
    73→- [x] Numbers in ascending numeric order: 5, 60.
    74→- [x] No duplicate numbers.
    75→- [x] Both 5 (combinatorics) and 60 (probability theory and stochastic processes) are valid AMS MSC2020 codes.
    76→
    77→## 9. Proof Bodies
    78→
    79→- [x] Uses `by\n  sorry` format (multi-line, acceptable per checklist): `:= by` on line 80, `sorry` indented on line 81.
    80→- [x] No `sorry` in `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations.
    81→- [x] No informal proof sketches for this open research problem.
    82→- [x] No `native_decide`.
    83→
    84→## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems
    85→
    86→- N/A: No variants present. The problem is a single question.
    87→
    88→## 11. Code Quality
    89→
    90→- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
    91→- [x] Unicode math symbols used where applicable: `ℕ`, `ℝ`, `∀`, `∃`, `≥`, `¬`, `↔`, `→`.
    92→- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
    93→- [x] No `set_option` that disables linters.
    94→- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all under 100 characters).
    95→- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.
    96→
    97→## 12. Build Verification
    98→
    99→- [ ] `lake build` not run during this review. No obvious issues; expected `sorry` warning only.
   100→- [ ] Custom linters not run during this review.
   101→
   102→---
   103→
   104→## Summary
   105→
   106→**Result: PASS** (all substantive items pass)
   107→
   108→The file is clean, well-structured, and fully conformant. The formalization is mathematically sound: using `Fin n → Fin n → Bool` as the sample space is equivalent to G(n, 1/2) since `toGraph625` only inspects entries above the diagonal, and each graph receives the same number of preimages, so the uniform distribution over Boolean matrices matches the G(n, 1/2) model (as the docstring on `toGraph625` correctly notes). The natural number subtraction `χ − ζ` is safe because every proper coloring is a valid cochromatic coloring (each color class is independent), so ζ(G) ≤ χ(G) always holds.
   109→
   110→Notes:
   111→1. **Build verification** (§12): Not performed during this review; should be confirmed in CI.
   112→

file.content:
# Formal Conjectures PR Checklist — `deepmind/625.lean`

Every item must pass before a PR is approved. Reviewers: reject if any box is unchecked
without an explicit justification.

---

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports`.
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erdős Problem 625`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to `erdosproblems.com/625`.
- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem (defines cochromatic number ζ(G) and chromatic number χ(G), asks whether χ(G) − ζ(G) → ∞ almost surely for G(n, 1/2)).
- [x] Bibliography entry present: `[ErGi93]` with author names and title.

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos625 ... end Erdos625`.
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase` (`Erdos625`).
- [x] `open` statements appear **before** the namespace block: `open SimpleGraph Finset` (line 36).

## 5. Custom Definitions

- [x] `toGraph625` (line 43) has a LaTeX docstring (lines 40–42).
- [x] `finChromaticNumber` (line 51) has a LaTeX docstring (lines 48–50).
- [x] `cochromaticNumber` (line 57) has a LaTeX docstring (lines 54–56).
- [x] `finChromaticNumber` is marked `noncomputable` (uses `sInf`, involving classical choice).
- [x] `cochromaticNumber` is marked `noncomputable` (uses `sInf`, involving classical choice).
- [x] `toGraph625` is computable (only uses `Bool` operations and decidable comparisons) — no `noncomputable` needed.
- [x] No `sorry` in any definition.
- N/A: No `Type`/`Prop` definitions to check for `UpperCamelCase`.
- [x] Functions returning values use `lowerCamelCase`: `toGraph625`, `finChromaticNumber`, `cochromaticNumber`.
- N/A: No `local notation` used (none needed).
- N/A: No `@[category test]`/`@[category API]` sanity checks (optional; "consider" item).

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Uses the `theorem` keyword.
- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_625` (snake_case, matches namespace number).
- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research open, AMS 5 60]`.
- [x] Main theorem has a docstring (lines 63–73) describing the problem and its formal interpretation.
- [x] Theorem docstring references source paper: `[ErGi93]`.

### `answer()` usage

The original problem asks "Is it true that almost surely χ(G) − ζ(G) → ∞?" — a yes/no question ("Is it true that ..."), so **Case 1** applies.

- [x] Theorem type is `answer(sorry) ↔ P` (line 75).
- [x] The RHS (`P`) formalizes the question as originally asked: for every M : ℕ and ε > 0, there exists n₀ such that for all n ≥ n₀, the fraction of Boolean edge matrices on `Fin n` whose graph has χ − ζ ≥ M is at least (1 − ε) times the total count. This correctly captures "almost surely χ − ζ → ∞" via the combinatorial/measure interpretation.
- [x] `answer(True)` would mean "yes" and `answer(False)` would mean "no" to the original question.
- [x] Quantifiers go **after** `answer(sorry)` (inside the RHS of `↔`), not as theorem parameters.

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[category ...]` attribute: `category research open`.
- [x] `research open` is a valid category value.
- [x] Theorem contains `sorry` in its proof body (consistent with `research open`).

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute: `AMS 5 60`.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed (two: 5 and 60).
- [x] Numbers in ascending numeric order: 5, 60.
- [x] No duplicate numbers.
- [x] Both 5 (combinatorics) and 60 (probability theory and stochastic processes) are valid AMS MSC2020 codes.

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Uses `by\n  sorry` format (multi-line, acceptable per checklist): `:= by` on line 80, `sorry` indented on line 81.
- [x] No `sorry` in `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations.
- [x] No informal proof sketches for this open research problem.
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

- N/A: No variants present. The problem is a single question.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used where applicable: `ℕ`, `ℝ`, `∀`, `∃`, `≥`, `¬`, `↔`, `→`.
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` that disables linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all under 100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] `lake build` not run during this review. No obvious issues; expected `sorry` warning only.
- [ ] Custom linters not run during this review.

---

## Summary

**Result: PASS** (all substantive items pass)

The file is clean, well-structured, and fully conformant. The formalization is mathematically sound: using `Fin n → Fin n → Bool` as the sample space is equivalent to G(n, 1/2) since `toGraph625` only inspects entries above the diagonal, and each graph receives the same number of preimages, so the uniform distribution over Boolean matrices matches the G(n, 1/2) model (as the docstring on `toGraph625` correctly notes). The natural number subtraction `χ − ζ` is safe because every proper coloring is a valid cochromatic coloring (each color class is independent), so ζ(G) ≤ χ(G) always holds.

Notes:
1. **Build verification** (§12): Not performed during this review; should be confirmed in CI.

content.text: The review passed — **Result: PASS**. No changes needed.
